title: PwD - Sort and View Product Listings - Functional Test Plan (33 Test Cases)
overview: Functional testing of SCRUM-76: PwD - Sort and View Product Listings. Validates that a PwD user can sort product listings using the Sort By dropdown, view product cards with all required information, and verify sort interactions, persistence, and edge cases.

testCases.title: Sort Dropdown Contains All Required Options
steps: Locate the 'Sort by' dropdown on the catalog page.
Click/open the dropdown.
Verify the available options.
testCases.preconditions: User is on the catalog landing page.
expectedResults: Dropdown lists all 6 sort options: Latest, Most Popular, Highest Rated, Price: Low to High, Price: High to Low, Name: A to Z.

testCases.title: Sort by Most Popular Reorders Results
steps: Note the first product displayed.
Select 'Most Popular' from the Sort by dropdown.
Observe the product order.
testCases.preconditions: User is on the catalog landing page with default sort.
expectedResults: Products are reordered by popularity.
Device count remains the same.

testCases.title: Sort by Highest Rated Reorders Results
steps: Select 'Highest Rated' from the Sort by dropdown.
Observe the first few product cards.
Verify device count remains the same as before sort.
testCases.preconditions: User is on the catalog landing page.
expectedResults: Products are reordered by rating.
Device count does not change.

testCases.title: Sort by Price: Low to High Reorders Results
steps: Select 'Price: Low to High' from the Sort by dropdown.
Observe the first few product cards.
Verify device count remains the same as before sort.
testCases.preconditions: User is on the catalog landing page.
expectedResults: Products are reordered by price ascending.
Device count does not change.

testCases.title: Sort by Price: High to Low Reorders Results
steps: Select 'Price: High to Low' from the Sort by dropdown.
Observe the first few product cards.
Verify device count remains the same as before sort.
testCases.preconditions: User is on the catalog landing page.
expectedResults: Products are reordered by price descending.
Device count does not change.

testCases.title: Sort by Alphabetical (A–Z) Reorders Results
steps: Select 'Name: A to Z' from the Sort by dropdown.
Observe the first few product cards.
Verify the first product's name comes alphabetically before the second.
testCases.preconditions: User is on the catalog landing page.
expectedResults: Products are ordered alphabetically A-Z.
First product name comes before the second alphabetically.

testCases.title: Sorting Refreshes Results Instantly (No Page Reload)
steps: Note the current product order.
Select a different sort option (e.g., 'Most Popular').
Observe the page behavior.
testCases.preconditions: User is on the catalog landing page.
expectedResults: Results reorder instantly without a full page reload.

testCases.title: Grid View Displays Product Cards in 2 Columns
steps: Observe the product grid layout.
Verify products are displayed in a 2-column grid on desktop viewport.
testCases.preconditions: User is on the catalog landing page at desktop viewport (1280x720).
expectedResults: Products are arranged in 2 columns per row.

testCases.title: Product Card Includes Product Image
steps: Observe the first product card.
Verify an image element is present within the card.
testCases.preconditions: User is on the catalog landing page.
expectedResults: Product card contains a visible image or background image.

testCases.title: Product Card Includes Product Name
steps: Observe the first product card.
Verify a heading (h3) with the product name is present.
testCases.preconditions: User is on the catalog landing page.
expectedResults: Product card contains a heading (h3) with the product name.

testCases.title: Product Card Includes Tags (Disability/Category)
steps: Observe the first product card.
Verify tags/badges are displayed.
testCases.preconditions: User is on the catalog landing page.
expectedResults: At least one tag/badge is visible on the product card.

testCases.title: Product Card Includes Rating (Stars + Review Count)
steps: Observe the first product card.
Verify a star rating element is present.
Verify a review count is displayed.
testCases.preconditions: User is on the catalog landing page.
expectedResults: Star rating is visible.
Review count is displayed.

testCases.title: Product Card Includes Short Description (Truncated)
steps: Observe the first product card.
Verify a short description text is present.
testCases.preconditions: User is on the catalog landing page.
expectedResults: A short description text is present on the product card.

testCases.title: Product Card Includes Key Features
steps: Observe the first product card.
Verify a 'Key Features' section or text is present.
testCases.preconditions: User is on the catalog landing page.
expectedResults: A 'Key Features' section or text is present on the product card.

testCases.title: Product Card Includes Availability Badge
steps: Observe the first product card.
Verify an availability badge is present.
testCases.preconditions: User is on the catalog landing page.
expectedResults: An availability badge (e.g., 'In Stock') is visible.

testCases.title: Product Card Price is Displayed
steps: Observe the first product card.
Verify a price is displayed (e.g., '₹899.99' or '₹12000').
testCases.preconditions: User is on the catalog landing page.
expectedResults: Price text is visible on the product card.

testCases.title: Product Card 'View Details' Link is Present and Clickable
steps: Observe the first product card.
Verify a 'View details' link is present.
Click the 'View details' link.
Observe the navigation.
testCases.preconditions: User is on the catalog landing page.
expectedResults: 'View details' link is visible.
Clicking opens product detail page or sign-in modal.

testCases.title: Pagination Available When Products Exceed Per-Page Limit
steps: Navigate to the catalog landing page with no filters.
Observe the pagination component below the product list.
Verify pagination controls are visible.
testCases.preconditions: Catalog has more products than the per-page limit.
expectedResults: Pagination controls are visible below the product list.

testCases.title: Default Sort Order on Page Load
steps: Navigate to the catalog landing page.
Observe the Sort by dropdown default value.
testCases.preconditions: User navigates to the catalog landing page.
expectedResults: Sort dropdown shows 'Latest' as the default.

testCases.title: Sort Dropdown Resets on Reset All
steps: Select 'Price: High to Low' from the Sort by dropdown.
Click 'Reset All'.
Observe the Sort by dropdown value.
testCases.preconditions: User has changed the sort order.
expectedResults: Sort dropdown reverts to 'Latest' after Reset All.

testCases.title: Sort Persists After Pagination Navigation
steps: Select 'Price: Low to High' from the Sort by dropdown.
Click 'Next' or page 2 in pagination.
Observe the Sort by dropdown value.
Observe the product order on page 2.
testCases.preconditions: User is on the catalog landing page with sort applied.
expectedResults: Sort dropdown retains selection after pagination.
Products on page 2 continue the sort order.

testCases.title: Sort Combined with Filters Works Correctly
steps: Select 'Device' from the Type filter dropdown.
Click 'Apply Filters'.
Select 'Highest Rated' from the Sort by dropdown.
Observe the results.
testCases.preconditions: User is on the catalog landing page.
expectedResults: Only filtered products are shown, sorted by selected option.
Device count remains same after sort.

testCases.title: Grid View Responsive on Mobile Viewport
steps: Open the catalog landing page on a mobile viewport.
Observe the product grid layout.
Verify products are displayed in a single column or adjusted layout.
testCases.preconditions: Browser viewport set to mobile (375x667).
expectedResults: Product cards stack in a single column.
No horizontal overflow.

testCases.title: Sort Order is Correct for Alphabetical (A–Z)
steps: Select 'Name: A to Z' from the Sort by dropdown.
Extract the name from the first product card.
Extract the name from the second product card.
Compare the two names alphabetically.
testCases.preconditions: User is on the catalog landing page.
expectedResults: First product name comes before or equals the second alphabetically.

testCases.title: Sort Dropdown is Keyboard Navigable
steps: Tab to the Sort by dropdown to focus it.
Press Enter to open the dropdown.
Use ArrowDown keys to navigate options.
Press Enter to select an option.
Verify the sort changes.
testCases.preconditions: User is on the catalog landing page.
expectedResults: Sort dropdown is fully operable via keyboard.
Selected sort option changes from default.

testCases.title: Rapid Sort Switching Returns Correct Final Results
steps: Rapidly switch sort from 'Most Popular' to 'Price: High to Low' to 'Name: A to Z' without waiting.
Wait for results to settle.
Verify the final sort selection and product order.
testCases.preconditions: User is on the catalog landing page.
expectedResults: Sort dropdown shows 'Name: A to Z'.
Products are in alphabetical order.

testCases.title: Sort Persists After Browser Back/Forward Navigation
steps: Select 'Most Popular' from the Sort by dropdown.
Navigate to page 2 via pagination.
Click browser Back button.
Verify the sort dropdown value.
testCases.preconditions: User is on the catalog landing page.
expectedResults: Sort dropdown retains selection after browser back navigation.

testCases.title: Product Cards Maintain Consistent Height in Grid
steps: Observe the first two product cards in the grid.
Compare their bounding box heights.
testCases.preconditions: User is on the catalog landing page with at least 2 product cards.
expectedResults: Cards in the same row have similar height (within 20px).

testCases.title: Sort Dropdown Visible on Mobile Viewport
steps: Set viewport to mobile (375x667).
Navigate to the catalog page.
Verify the Sort by dropdown is visible.
Verify all sort options are available.
testCases.preconditions: Browser viewport set to mobile (375x667).
expectedResults: Sort dropdown is visible on mobile.
All 6 sort options are available.

testCases.title: No Horizontal Overflow on Any Sort Option
steps: Cycle through all 6 sort options.
After each sort, check the page body scroll width.
Verify no horizontal overflow occurs.
testCases.preconditions: User is on the catalog landing page.
expectedResults: No horizontal overflow for any sort option.

testCases.title: Price Sort Validates Actual Price Ordering (Low to High)
steps: Select 'Price: Low to High' from the Sort by dropdown.
Extract prices from the first 3 product cards.
Compare each consecutive pair.
testCases.preconditions: User is on the catalog landing page.
expectedResults: Each product's price is >= the previous product's price.

testCases.title: Price Sort Validates Actual Price Ordering (High to Low)
steps: Select 'Price: High to Low' from the Sort by dropdown.
Extract prices from the first 3 product cards.
Compare each consecutive pair.
testCases.preconditions: User is on the catalog landing page.
expectedResults: Each product's price is <= the previous product's price.

testCases.title: Multiple Sort Changes Do Not Duplicate Product Cards
steps: Note the initial product card count on the page.
Switch sort to 'Most Popular', then 'Price: Low to High', then 'Name: A to Z'.
Compare the final product card count to the initial count.
testCases.preconditions: User is on the catalog landing page.
expectedResults: Product card count remains the same after multiple sort changes.
